satire_attempt: Senior engineer announces that the conformance test suite is now the soul of the system. Junior pushes back: 'isn't that just integration tests?' Senior smiles in the way of one who has read Krishna. Six months later, the team has 11,000 conformance tests, 3,000 of them flaky, and has shipped nothing. But they have located the imperishable.
verdict_reason: The satire only works by inventing an excess (11,000 tests, 3,000 flaky) that the verse does not endorse. The verse's claim — that conformance is the diagnostic of whether continuity has actually been preserved — is too specific to mock without disagreeing with it. The falsifiability section explicitly addresses the 'asserting continuity without verifying' failure mode that the satire tries to invert. PASS.
